findings.description: Rule is classed color_application only but the source unit bundles distinct layout specs (2px rule weight, 60-100% rule width, 16px padding) with the color/typography directive. These are separable governance facets (color vs. spacing/layout) that get lost under a single color_application class.

payload.note: extract rule-weight/width/padding spec into a layout-class rule linked to rule_lisraya_main_callout_style

findings.description: Secondary callout rule bundles box-tint/color directive (must) with a general internal padding spec and a cross-referenced corner-radius rule, both layout concerns, under color_application class only.

payload.note: extract 20px padding + corner-radius reference into separate layout rule

findings.description: 'corner radius per accent-shape rules §2.5' is an explicit cross-document governance reference that is not captured as a relation to any accent-shape rule group; the dependency is silently dropped.
Source says icon inclusion is optional ('may include one line icon') but the rule is flagged with blanket polarity=must, conflating the mandatory color/box spec with an optional icon element.

payload.note: add optional/may sub-facet for icon usage distinct from must polarity on box/text color

findings.description: Text-heavy callout rule mixes multiple mandatory color/typography directives (headline, subhead, body colors) plus a responsive layout rule (two-column collapsing to single column on mobile) under one color_application rule, obscuring the distinct scope (desktop vs mobile) of the layout clause.

payload.note: extract responsive column-layout clause with explicit mobile/desktop scope

findings.description: Rule is classed as layout, but the source clause ('never two of the same style competing on one screen/section') is a usage/governance constraint on style selection frequency, not a spatial/layout spec; class mismatch may misroute this rule in layout-specific validation.
